definitions: Deducting an amount from the usual price of something.
Regarding something as being of less importance or value.
Allowing for the unreliability of a source or account.
Selling or offering for sale at a reduced price.
examples: The store is discounting all winter coats.
She was discounting his advice, thinking it was irrelevant.
He was discounting the rumors as mere speculation.
The company is discounting its older models to clear inventory.
